Class BuWizz3Dsl

java.lang.Object
ch.varani.bricks.ble.api.dsl.BuWizz3Dsl
public final class BuWizz3Dsl extends Object
Fluent DSL sub-builder for BuWizz 3.0 protocol operations.

Obtained via ConnectionDsl.asBuWizz3(). Commands are written to the Application characteristic (BuWizz3ProtocolConstants.APPLICATION_CHARACTERISTIC_UUID) within the Application service.

Usage example: 

connectionDsl.asBuWizz3()
    .setPowerLevel(BuWizz3ProtocolConstants.CMD_SET_MOTOR_DATA)
    .setMotorData(100, -100, 0, 0, 0, 0, false, false, false, false, false, false)
    .setLed(0, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x00)
    .done();

Thread safety: not thread-safe; do not share across threads.

DSL maintenance rule: any change to BuWizz3ProtocolConstants must be reflected here. See AGENTS.md §16.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    activateShelfMode()
    Activates shelf mode (0xA1).
    @NonNull BleConnection

    Returns the underlying BleConnection.
    void
    done()
    Disconnects the BuWizz 3.0 brick and releases all resources.
    @NonNull Flow.Publisher<byte[]>
    notifications()
    Returns a publisher that emits BuWizz 3.0 status report packets at approximately 20 Hz (command 0x01).
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    resetLeds()
    Reverts all LEDs to default behaviour by sending the command byte alone.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setAllLeds(int r1, int g1, int b1, int r2, int g2, int b2, int r3, int g3, int b3, int r4, int g4, int b4)
    Sends a Set LED Status command (0x36) for all four motor LEDs.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setCurrentLimits(int limit1, int limit2, int limit3, int limit4, int limit5, int limit6)
    Sends a Set Current Limits command (0x38) for all 6 channels.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setDataTransferPeriod(int periodMs)
    Sends a Set Data Transfer Period command (0x32).
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setDeviceName(@NonNull String name)
    Sets the BuWizz 3.0 device name (0x20).
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setLedsUniform(int red, int green, int blue)
    Sets all four LEDs to the same colour.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setMotorData(int s1, int s2, int s3, int s4, int s5, int s6, boolean brake1, boolean brake2, boolean brake3, boolean brake4, boolean brake5, boolean brake6)
    Sends a Set Motor Data command (0x30) for all 6 channels.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setMotorTimeout(int configuration)
    Sends a Set Motor Timeout command (0x34).
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setPuPortFunctions(int func1, int func2, int func3, int func4)
    Sends a Set PU Port Function command (0x50) for all 4 PU ports.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    setWatchdog(int timeoutSeconds)
    Activates or deactivates the connection watchdog (0x35).
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    stopAllMotors()
    Sends a Set Motor Data command with all channels stopped and coast mode.

  • Method Details

    • notifications

      public @NonNull Flow.Publisher<byte[]> notifications()
      Returns a publisher that emits BuWizz 3.0 status report packets at approximately 20 Hz (command 0x01).
      Returns:
      a publisher of status report payloads; never null

    • setMotorData

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setMotorData(int s1, int s2, int s3, int s4, int s5, int s6, boolean brake1, boolean brake2, boolean brake3, boolean brake4, boolean brake5, boolean brake6)
      Sends a Set Motor Data command (0x30) for all 6 channels.

      Speed values are signed bytes where: BuWizz3ProtocolConstants.MOTOR_SPEED_FULL_REVERSE = full reverse, BuWizz3ProtocolConstants.MOTOR_SPEED_STOP = stop, BuWizz3ProtocolConstants.MOTOR_SPEED_FULL_FORWARD = full forward.

      Parameters:
      s1 - signed speed for channel 1 (−127 to 127)
      s2 - signed speed for channel 2
      s3 - signed speed for channel 3
      s4 - signed speed for channel 4
      s5 - signed speed for channel 5
      s6 - signed speed for channel 6
      brake1 - true = slow-decay on channel 1
      brake2 - true = slow-decay on channel 2
      brake3 - true = slow-decay on channel 3
      brake4 - true = slow-decay on channel 4
      brake5 - true = slow-decay on channel 5
      brake6 - true = slow-decay on channel 6
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • stopAllMotors

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> stopAllMotors()
      Sends a Set Motor Data command with all channels stopped and coast mode.
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setDataTransferPeriod

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setDataTransferPeriod(int periodMs)
      Sends a Set Data Transfer Period command (0x32).

      Valid range: 20–255 ms in steps of 5 ms.

      Parameters:
      periodMs - the desired notification period in milliseconds
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setMotorTimeout

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setMotorTimeout(int configuration)
      Sends a Set Motor Timeout command (0x34).

      Configuration values:

      • 0 — stop immediately and brake
      • 1 — stop immediately and coast
      • 2–254 — coast to stop in (N-1) seconds
      Parameters:
      configuration - the timeout configuration byte
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setWatchdog

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setWatchdog(int timeoutSeconds)
      Activates or deactivates the connection watchdog (0x35).

      A value of 0 disables the watchdog. On expiry, the device drops the connection and applies the motor-timeout stop configuration.

      Parameters:
      timeoutSeconds - watchdog timeout in seconds; 0 = disabled
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setAllLeds

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setAllLeds(int r1, int g1, int b1, int r2, int g2, int b2, int r3, int g3, int b3, int r4, int g4, int b4)
      Sends a Set LED Status command (0x36) for all four motor LEDs.
      Parameters:
      r1 - red channel of LED 1 (0–255)
      g1 - green channel of LED 1 (0–255)
      b1 - blue channel of LED 1 (0–255)
      r2 - red channel of LED 2
      g2 - green channel of LED 2
      b2 - blue channel of LED 2
      r3 - red channel of LED 3
      g3 - green channel of LED 3
      b3 - blue channel of LED 3
      r4 - red channel of LED 4
      g4 - green channel of LED 4
      b4 - blue channel of LED 4
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setLedsUniform

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setLedsUniform(int red, int green, int blue)
      Sets all four LEDs to the same colour.
      Parameters:
      red - red component (0–255)
      green - green component (0–255)
      blue - blue component (0–255)
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • resetLeds

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> resetLeds()
      Reverts all LEDs to default behaviour by sending the command byte alone.
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setCurrentLimits

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setCurrentLimits(int limit1, int limit2, int limit3, int limit4, int limit5, int limit6)
      Sends a Set Current Limits command (0x38) for all 6 channels.

      Each limit is expressed in steps of 30 mA. Default on connect: 1.5 A for ports 1–4 (value 50), 3.0 A for ports 5–6 (value 100).

      Parameters:
      limit1 - current limit for channel 1
      limit2 - current limit for channel 2
      limit3 - current limit for channel 3
      limit4 - current limit for channel 4
      limit5 - current limit for channel 5
      limit6 - current limit for channel 6
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setDeviceName

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setDeviceName(@NonNull String name)
      Sets the BuWizz 3.0 device name (0x20).

      The name is NUL-terminated and truncated to 12 ASCII characters.

      Parameters:
      name - the desired device name (max 12 ASCII characters)
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setPuPortFunctions

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setPuPortFunctions(int func1, int func2, int func3, int func4)
      Sends a Set PU Port Function command (0x50) for all 4 PU ports.
      Parameters:
      func1 - port function for port 1 (see PU_PORT_FUNCTION_* constants)
      func2 - port function for port 2
      func3 - port function for port 3
      func4 - port function for port 4
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • activateShelfMode

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> activateShelfMode()
      Activates shelf mode (0xA1).

      The device disconnects the battery immediately. It wakes only when a charger is connected.

      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • done

      public void done() throws BleException
      Disconnects the BuWizz 3.0 brick and releases all resources.
      Throws:
      BleException - if the disconnect fails

    • connection

      public @NonNull BleConnection connection()
      Returns the underlying BleConnection.
      Returns:
      the connection; never null
